content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree on NNN vertices. The tree is rooted at vertex RRR.

For each uuu from 111 to NNN, we define the value GuG_uGu‚Äã as follows:


GuG_uGu‚Äã is the **maximum** value of gcd‚Å°(u,v)\gcd(u, v)gcd(u,v) across all vertices v<uv \lt uv<u that lie in the subtree‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ of uuu.

If no vertex with a label smaller than uuu lies within its subtree, GuG_uGu‚Äã is defined to be ‚àí1-1‚àí1 instead.
Compute all the NNN values G1,G2,‚Ä¶,GNG_1, G_2, \ldots, G_NG1‚Äã,G2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,GN‚Äã.

‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ Vertex vvv is said to lie in the subtree of vertex uuu, if uuu is present on the unique path between vvv and the root.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî  the number of vertices in the tree.
The second line of each test case contains a single integer RRR ‚Äî the root of the tree.
The next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines describe the edges of the tree. Each of them contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between these two vertices.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers: the values G1,G2,‚Ä¶,GNG_1, G_2, \ldots, G_NG1‚Äã,G2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,GN‚Äã.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree on {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}.
The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

444 has only 222 smaller than it inside its subtree, so G2=gcd‚Å°(2,4)=2G_2 = \gcd(2, 4) = 2G2‚Äã=gcd(2,4)=2.
